_meta.document: Andhra Pradesh Building Rules 2012 — G.O.Ms.No.168, Dated 07-04-2012
applicable_bylaws: National Building Code 2005 / NBC 2016 Part IV (Fire & Life Safety)
_meta.notes: DRAFT — values extracted from G.O.Ms.No.168 (AP Building Rules 2012) and GHMC/HMDA zoning regulations. FAR values from GHMC Master Plan Zoning Regulations. VERIFY all fields against official gazette before use in production.

zones._note: GHMC/HMDA Zoning categories per Master Plan 2031 Zoning Regulations. FAR values from GHMC Master Plan — VERIFY against official gazette before production use.

MU2.label: Mixed Use — High Intensity (Near Metro / ORR)

ground_coverage_pct._note: Maximum ground coverage by plot size. GHMC norms.
ground_coverage_pct._status: VERIFY — confirm exact thresholds from GHMC building permission rules.

setbacks_non_high_rise._note: Setbacks for Non-High Rise buildings (height < 18m). Source: Table III, Rule 5, G.O.Ms.No.168.
setbacks_non_high_rise._status: VERIFY — sourced from Table III of AP Building Rules 2012.
setbacks_non_high_rise._road_width_keys: Road width upper boundary in metres: 12=up to 12m, 18=12 to 18m, 24=18 to 24m, 30=24 to 30m, 9999=above 30m

setbacks_high_rise._note: All-round setbacks for High Rise buildings (height ≥ 18m). Source: Table IV, Rule 7, G.O.Ms.No.168.
setbacks_high_rise._status: VERIFY — sourced from Table IV of AP Building Rules 2012.
setbacks_high_rise._key_note: Height keys are upper boundary in metres. All-round setback applies to sides and rear; front setback = max(road-width setback from Table III, all-round setback).

minimum_road_width._note: Minimum abutting road width for various building types. Source: Table II, Rule 4, G.O.Ms.No.168.
minimum_road_width._status: VERIFY — sourced from Table II of AP Building Rules 2012.

parking._note: Parking area as percentage of total built-up area. Source: Table V, Rule 13, G.O.Ms.No.168. Values for GHMC/HMDA Area.
parking._status: VERIFY — sourced from Table V of AP Building Rules 2012.
notes: Parking in basements/cellars allowed for plots ≥ 750 sqm (one or more levels)
Stilt floor for parking: entire stilt may satisfy requirement for plots ≤ 750 sqm
Up to 10% of cellar may be used for utilities (AC plant, generator, STP, electrical)

fire_safety._note: Fire NOC and safety requirements per Rule 5(f)(xv), Rule 7, Rule 15, G.O.Ms.No.168 and NBC 2005 / AP Fire Services Act 1999.
fire_safety._status: VERIFY — confirm height thresholds from AP State Disasters Response & Fire Services Dept rules.
requirements_above_15m_commercial: Prior clearance from AP State Disasters Response & Fire Services Department
Fire escape staircase and lift (in addition to regular staircase/lift)
NBC 2005 Part IV Fire Protection requirements
requirements_above_18m: Fire NOC from AP State Disasters Response & Fire Services Department mandatory
At least one fire escape staircase and fire lift in addition to regular
NBC 2005 / NBC 2016 Part IV full fire protection compliance
Smoke containment and smoke management measures
Fire engineer / fire consultant to certify installations
NOC renewal required from time to time

staircase_lift._note: Staircase and lift requirements per NBC 2005 and Rule 15 of G.O.Ms.No.168.
staircase_lift._status: VERIFY — confirm staircase width and lift trigger from NBC 2005.
notes: All High Rise buildings must have at least one fire escape staircase and one fire lift (Rule 15(b)(i))
Staircase design per NBC 2005 Part III Section 4

basement._status: VERIFY — confirm setback and use restrictions from Rule 13.
permitted_uses: Up to 10% for utilities: AC plant, generator, STP, electrical installations, laundry
notes: Cellar floor shall be used only for parking and not for any habitation purpose (Rule 13(c)(iii))
Visitors' parking (min 10% of required parking) may be in mandatory setbacks (not front) where setback > 6m

projections._note: Projections in setbacks per Rule 6, G.O.Ms.No.168.
projections.balcony_projection_non_high_rise: not permitted in mandatory setbacks for non-high-rise
notes: Chajjas/weather shades max 60 cm in mandatory setbacks (Rule 6a)
No balcony projections in setbacks for non-high-rise buildings (Rule 6b)
Balcony up to 2m may project into open space for upper floors (>6m) in high-rise (Rule 7a xiv)
Security guard booth of 2 sqm allowed in front setback for individual residential plots > 300 sqm

group_development._note: Group Development Scheme norms per Rule 8, G.O.Ms.No.168.
notes: Minimum 10% of site as organised open space (Rule 8g)
Developer to provide 20% of land for EWS/LIG housing for sites ≥ 4000 sqm in HMDA area (Rule 11)

locations: All along the Inner Ring Road outside erstwhile MCH limits
Along River Musi outside erstwhile MCH limits

bylaw_thresholds._note: Key height/area thresholds that trigger different regulatory tiers.

accessibility._source: AP Building Rules 2012, Rule 15.a.v & Annexure-V — NBC 2005 Part-III Clause 12.21 (pages 312–321)
accessibility.applicability: All public buildings and facilities used by the public; includes temporary/emergency conditions. Does NOT apply to private residences.

door.note: Self-closing doors: force minimised; sliding automatic preferred

staircase.note: Consistent height/depth throughout; avoid projecting nosings and open stairs

controls.note: All controls (switches, intercoms, etc.) must be within this zone

handrails.note: Circular section ~40mm diameter; colour contrast with wall behind; ~50mm curb at exposed sides of ramps

solar._source: AP Building Rules 2012, Rule 15.a.xi (page 21) and Rule 22 (page 26)
mandatory_for: Group Housing Buildings with 100 units and above
requirements: Solar Water Heating System in the building
Solar Lighting System in the building and on site for outdoor lighting
solar.bank_guarantee_note: Applicant shall give bank guarantee to sanctioning authority for compliance
solar.incentive_clause: Rule 22(i) — 10% rebate on property tax for installing and using solar heating and lighting system

water_recycling._source: AP Building Rules 2012, Rule 15.a.xii (page 21) and Rule 22 (page 26)
mandatory_for: Group Housing Buildings with 100 units and above
water_recycling.requirement: Provision for Recycling of Water shall be made
water_recycling.incentive_clause: Rule 22(ii) — 10% rebate on property tax for undertaking BOTH recycling of waste water AND rainwater harvesting structures

rainwater_harvesting._source: AP Building Rules 2012, Rule 15.a.vii & Annexure-VI (G.O.Ms.No.350 M.A., Dated 09-06-2000), pages 322–324
rainwater_harvesting.applicable_to: ALL buildings in all Municipal Corporations, Urban Development Authorities, and Municipalities
rainwater_harvesting.technical_specs_note: Plots 300 sqm and above: Part-A compulsory; Part-B, C or D optional
filter_media_layers: 40mm road metals — bottom layer up to 50% of depth
20mm road metal — lower middle layer up to 20% of depth
Coarse sand — upper middle layer up to 20% of depth
rainwater_harvesting.terrace_connection: Terrace connected to sump or wall through filtering tank by PVC pipe; valve to discharge first dirty rainwater to ground
penalty_acts: Section 41 of A.P. Urban Development Authority Act, 1975
Section 596 & 597 of Hyderabad Municipal Corporation Act, 1955
rainwater_harvesting.incentive: 10% property tax rebate when BOTH recycling of waste water AND rainwater harvesting structures are undertaken (Rule 22.ii)

sanction_clearance.initial_fee_note: Collected with application. Balance fees due before permission issued. If application rejected, initial fee forfeited.

approval_authorities.GHMC: Chief City Planner / Town Planning Section Head (Convener) — Commissioner, GHMC
approval_authorities.HMDA_UDA: Head of Town Planning Wing of UDA (Convener) — Metropolitan Commissioner / Vice Chairman
approval_authorities.Other_Corps_Municipalities: Town Planning Section Head (Convener) — Director of Town & Country Planning
approval_authorities.Gram_Panchayats: Regional Deputy Director of Town Planning (Convener) — Director of Town & Country Planning

mandatory_documents_above_10m: Structural designs and drawings certified by licensed Structural Engineer (Rule 5.f.xviii.b)
Plans signed by Owner, Builder, Architect, and Structural Engineer (Rule 5.f.xviii.c)
Registered agreement between owner and builder, if applicable (Rule 5.f.xviii.d)
Undertaking on Rs.100 stamp paper — no possession without Occupancy Certificate (Rule 5.f.xviii.e)
All-Risks Insurance Policy for construction period (Rule 5.f.xviii.f)
mandatory_clearances_high_rise: Fire NOC from AP State Disasters Response & Fire Services Dept (Rule 7.a.vi)
Airport Authority clearance if within restricted / air funnel zone (Rule 7.a.v)

occupancy_certificate.exception: Individual buildings on plots up to 100 sqm with height up to 7m — optional
occupancy_certificate.partial_oc_condition: Flats/units fulfilling all requirements plus basic facilities (lifts, water supply, sanitation, drainage, roads, common lighting)
occupancy_certificate.height_tolerance_note: Total height may vary up to 1m with no change in permitted floors, subject to fire service norms
occupancy_certificate.high_rise_extra: OC issued ONLY after fire safety clearance from AP State Disasters Response & Fire Services Dept
penalties_without_oc: Utility agencies (electricity, water, drainage, sewerage) must deny regular connections OR charge 3x tariff until OC produced
Local Body must collect 2x property tax annually from owner / occupier
Registration Authority: register ONLY permitted built-up area per sanctioned plan
Financial agencies: extend loans ONLY to permitted built-up area

non_high_rise_above_750sqm.use: Greenery, tot lot, or soft landscaping

chowk_inner_courtyard.note: Allowed above stilt floor; must be open to sky

inter_block_spacing.note: Space between two blocks must be >= all-round setback of tallest block (Table IV); NOT counted as organised open space

height_exemptions._source: AP Building Rules 2012, Rule 2.e and Rule 2.f — Definitions (page 3)
excluded_from_high_rise_classification: Cold storage and other non-working areas (industrial buildings)
